question_text: Water boils at 100°C at sea level. A student observes that 2 kg of water takes 10 minutes to boil away completely once it reaches 100°C. How many kilograms of water would you expect to boil away in 5 minutes at the same conditions?
explanation: If 2 kg boils away in 10 minutes, the rate of boiling is 2 ÷ 10 = 0.2 kg per minute. In 5 minutes, the mass that boils away is 0.2 × 5 = 1 kg. This assumes the conditions (heat source, container) remain constant.
